Predeclared batch checks
Evidence boundary: This report measures modeled operational-loss avoidance in a controlled matched recovery harness. It is not realized customer revenue. No market prediction, trading, fee selection, account access, or electrical-energy claim is included.

Executive result

Program 32 Full processed the same matched workload and physical capacity as the Strong controller across 42 complete independent ten-minute rounds. The cumulative modeled advantage was $16,902.74, producing a modeled Glyph Systems 50% share of $8,451.37 and an aggregate rate of $20.0645 per minute.

The complete-round distribution had a median of $4.8222/min, a 10th percentile of $0.7794/min, and a worst round of $0.5340/min.

Methodology and fairness

Each complete round lasted ten minutes: twenty seconds of clean Anchor calibration, randomized active distortion and recovery, and a one-minute final clean drain. Profile order was balanced in randomized blocks so every four rounds contained LOW, MODERATE, HIGH, and BURST_SATURATION once.

All five controllers received identical transfers, hidden service outcomes, fault schedule, physical service ceiling, queue limit, retry limit, idempotency capability, visible state source, restoration timing, and frozen cost coefficients. Controllers differed only in scheduling, holding, verification, retry timing, quarantine, memory, and recovery interpretation.

The persistent-memory track carried only clean Anchor and smoothed memory estimates between rounds. Transactions, queues, active distress, tension, residue, Stillness state, and release state reset every round.
